title: PEER-TO-PEER SUPPORT SERVICES - 211info
text: CASCADE AIDS PROJECT PEER-TO-PEER SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides supportive services for people living with HIV/AIDS, mental health challenges, or substance use issues. Helps individuals connect with healthcare, mental health, and drug and alcohol recovery services 520 NW Davis Street Suite 215 Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals with an HIV/AIDS diagnosis, income at or below 300% of the federal poverty level, needing extra mental health or substance use support, currently housed but struggling to maintain housing, or needing help connecting with services. Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm Email (503) 223-5907 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 223-5907 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Multiple TriMet MAX and bus lines within the block Services: Peer Counseling * AIDS/HIV Peer Mental Health Support Services * AIDS/HIV Other Services Offered At This Location: HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - TESTING4ALL HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - CAMP KC HOUSING/SHELTER - CAP HOUSING SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - DROP IN CENTER If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CASCADE AIDS PROJECT
